title: The real cost of building with AI vs. traditional dev team
post:
Let's talk numbers. What does it actually cost to build a SaaS in 2025?

Traditional team (2 engineers + 1 designer): $800K/year minimum. Eight months to MVP. Plus hiring time, onboarding, retention.

AI-augmented solo founder: $50K/year in tools. Three months to MVP. No hiring, no management, no HR nightmares.

The quality gap is smaller than you think for early-stage products. The speed gap is massive.

For anything past MVP, the calculus changes. You need humans for complex architecture, customer relationships, and things AI can't do yet. But getting to MVP? The math is undeniable.

I've built three products this way. None made me rich, but all shipped. And shipping beats not shipping.

The question isn't whether AI development is cheaper. It is. The question is whether it produces better outcomes.

What's your development cost structure? Thoughts?
